MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Daniel Kalinowski of the knight Law Group, Tel. No. 1-310-552-2250 appearing
for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. Barbara R. Adams of Clark Hillm Tel. No. 1-415-984-8544 appearing for the
Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants FCA US LLC and Michael Stead's Auto Depot, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff John Cox, Jr.'s request for Civil Code 3345 treble penalties is granted. Mr. Cox's supplemental interrogatory responses dated April 2, 2018 show that Mr. Cox does not now have and is unable to obtain any evidence that: 1) at the time Mr. Cox made his request to buyback his 2011 Jeep Grand Cherokee, either defendant knew or should have known his age and 2) defendants' alleged wrongful refusal to buyback the Jeep caused Mr. Cox to suffer any of the losses identified in Civil Code 3345(b)(2). Based on those responses, defendants satisfied their summary adjudication burden. Because Mr. Cox failed to identify any facts creating a triable dispute on either of these issues, defendants are entitled to summary adjudication in their favor on Mr. Cox's request for Civil Code 3345 treble penalties. Mr. Cox presented no evidence that either defendant knew of his age when made his buyback request. Mr. Cox's appearance and the fact that he was retired are insufficient bases to infer that he was a senior citizen at the time he made his buyback request. Nor has Mr. Cox provided any evidence that there was any reason why defendants could or should have reviewed any references to Mr. Cox's age in documentation received by them at or around the time of Mr. Cox's purchase of the Jeep when several years later he made his buyback request and did not make any mention of his age. Moreover, Mr. Cox's purchase of the Jeep when he was not a "senior citizen" does not constitute a section 3345(b)(2) loss. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-22-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ruling: PLAINTIFF JOHN COX, MARIA COX Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint is granted on specified conditions which plaintiffs must agree to either orally at the hearing or in a writing sent to counsel for defendants prior to the hearing. The conditions that plaintiffs must agree to are: 1) the trial is continued to August 6, 2018 at 9:30am in department 206; 2) plaintiffs must serve verified code-compliant responses without objection other than privilege no later than April 2, 2018 to the following deem-served discovery: a) state all facts in support of all new allegations; b) state the name and contact information for each person who knows any of those facts and what facts each person knows; c) identify all documents which refer to or memorialize such facts and produce them; 3) plaintiffs must make all persons identified in the responses available for a deposition of no more than two hours in length no later than April 15, 2018; and 4) defendants may file a summary adjudication motion directed to all new allegations on 30 days' notice. If plaintiffs do not agree to all of these conditions, the motion is denied. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The conditions on the granting of the motion eliminate any legal prejudice to defendants by the granting of the motion. Order signed after hearing. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600; Dept. 302.
